senses.gloss: to be or become short, to shorten
to fall short of, to miss
to unable, to be incapable of
to desist, to refrain, to abstain
to shorten, to curtail, to pare
to detain, to lock up, to pen up, to put in jail
to hold back, to keep or restrict (within certain limits), to restrain
to confine to, to limit to, to restrict to
to shorten; to abbreviate; to clip (hair)
to fall short, to shirk one's responsibilities, to not pull one's weight
to lag behind, to be slothful
large stone building from which power is projected, castle, palace
to wash, to scour, to full, to walk
to wash, to scour, to full, to walk

etymology.text:
From Aramaic קַצְרָא (qaṣrā), from Latin castrum. Cognate to Classical Syriac ܩܰܨܪܳܐ (qaṣrā), Hebrew קְצָרָה (qəṣārâ), Byzantine Greek κάστρον (kástron).

Back-formed from قَصَّار (qaṣṣār, “walker, fuller”).

body.text: (a thing, S, Msb, i. e. anything, M)
The arrow fell short of the butt; did not reach it;
fell upon the ground without reaching the butt:
he fell short of his place of alighting
came, short of doing the thing,
he could not attain to it:
(S:) or the first has this signification; (ISk, S, Msb;) and [in like manner]
abstained from it, being unable to do
abstained from it, being able to do
(ISk, S, M, Msb:) such, at least, is generally the case, though both sometimes occur in one and the same sense, that which
it signifies nearly the same as
he fell short of accomplishing the affair; he fell short of doing what was requisite,
what he ought to have done,
a meaning very common, and implied, though not expressed, in the M: and]
part thereof, with ability to do it.
fell short of what we required;
did not enable us to attain our object;
(Msb;) meaning, that they were unable to pay the expenses: (Mgh:) and
his hope fell short of what he required
but to-day, hope hath fallen short of extending to the meeting with thee
wish, fell short of what was requisite with respect to such a thing
], said to him who has sought, or desired, little, and a mean share or lot. (TA.) And,
He fell short of what was required by such a one,
sparingly, with such a one, in a gift.

. (S, M, Msb, K. *) It is said in a trad. of Mo'ádh,
To him belongeth what he hath held confined in,
what he hath held in possession

kept safe the girl by means of the veil,
and in like manner you say of a horse. (TA.) And in a trad. of 'Omar it is said,
namely a company of riders upon camels on other beasts. (L, TA.) You also say

that he desired to do. (TA.) [See an ex. in a verse cited voce

eyes, towards that at which I ought not to look.

body.text: because of the property of the orphans
which they might leave]. (TA.) And one says
restricted, myself to the thing, and obliged myself to do it.
(TA.) [See also 8.] Hence what is said of Thumámeh, in a trad.,
But he refused to become a Muslim by constraint and compulsion:
, as is done in many other cases. (TA.) You say also
I restricted the thing to such a thing.
He reduced him, to the thing,
that I might drink her milk. (Msb.) Aboo-Du-ád says, describing a horse,

coming, short of accomplishing an affair;
you say to a man whom you have sent to accomplish some needful affair, and who has fallen short of doing what you commanded him to do, on account of heat or some other cause,
